violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Once food safety certificate is obtained, establishment to re-instate Rhode Island Certified Manager in food Safety license. Application provided during time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/04/2026
Insectide and insectide tape were observed in the establishment. PIC informed to cease practice. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026
Deceased cockroaches were observed on glue traps. Deceased pests to be removed to deter attractants for other pests. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: -storage shelving -interior and exterior of reach-in coolers New Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2026

The facility is lacking a mop sink. At least one mop sink or one curbed cleaning facility equipped with a floor drain must be provided and conveniently located for the cleaning of mops or similar wet floor cleaning tools and for the disposal of mop water and similar liquid waste. Establishment is using sink in staff restroom as a handwashing sink and mop sink. New Violation. Correct By: 10/21/2026

The floors under equipment have an accumulation of soil residue, old rodent droppings and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2026

The kitchen hood (vents and interior) have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris.) in The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

The establishment's Rhode Island Food Safety Manager license is expired. ServSafe food safety certificate is current. Application provided during time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2025

Various foods in the reach-in coolers were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025
Insectide and insectide tape were observed in the establishment. PIC informed to cease practice. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025

The floors under the reach-in freezers have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2025
